sections.paragraph:
ὑπάρχω [ὑπό, ἄρχω]

—1. ‘be present in a functional manner’, be there, take place 1 Cor 11:18; have (its/their) place Phil 3:20; 2 Pt 1:8; be at (one’s) disposal Ac 3:6; 4:37; belong (to) 4:34; 10:12; 28:7; 2 Pt 1:8; sim. Lk 8:3; 12:15; Ac 4:32. The ptc. use in many of the preceding passages finds expression in the nominal formation τὰ ὑπάρχοντα property, holdings Mt 19:21; 24:47; 25:14; Lk 11:21; 12:33, 44; 14:33; 16:1; 19:8; 1 Cor 13:3; Hb 10:34; sim. Ac 5:4 ‘it was your property’; 2 Pt 3:11.

—2. ‘to function or be in a state as determined by circumstance’, be: of occupying a place or position Lk 8:41; 9:48; Ac 2:30; 2 Pt 2:19; of characteristic Ac 7:55; Gal 1:14; of condition Ac 8:16; Ro 4:19; 1 Cor 7:26; 2 Cor 8:17; Phil 2:6 (the form of God); Js 2:15; of status or relation Ac 16:3; 19:31 v.l.
